claim: The United States formally implemented a naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz beginning at 10 a.m. EDT on April 13, 2026.
President Trump announced that the U.S. Navy would intercept any ships paying transit tolls to Iran to choke off Tehran's maritime revenue.
Trump claimed Iranian naval capability has been significantly degraded and Iran is in very bad shape.
The U.S. military deployed the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier, 11 destroyers, and the USS Tripoli amphibious group to enforce the blockade.
Two guided-missile destroyers, USS Frank E. Peterson and USS Michael Murphy, crossed the Strait of Hormuz on April 13 as part of mine-clearing operations.
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps issued warnings against U.S. miscalculations, threatening that one wrong move would plunge U.S. forces into a deadly whirlpool of destruction.
The IRGC Navy claimed the Strait of Hormuz remained under Iran's full control and warned that military vessels would receive a forceful response.
Iranian state TV released footage depicting a tense radio exchange between an IRGC naval station and a U.S. Navy destroyer near the Strait of Hormuz.
Peace talks in Islamabad over the weekend failed after 21 hours of negotiations, with Vice President JD Vance citing Iran's refusal to abandon nuclear ambitions as the main sticking point.
Pakistan is pushing to resume US-Iran talks before the current ceasefire expires around April 22, 2026.
The United Kingdom announced it would not enforce the U.S. blockade, with Prime Minister Keir Starmer stating the strait must reopen with no conditions and no tolls.
The U.K. deployed additional military planners to U.S. Central Command to explore post-conflict operations.
Iran perceives itself as holding leverage amid US dysfunction and chaos and believes it is winning by objective standards.
Pakistan is facilitating indirect US-Iran talks due to its ties with the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps.
A fragile two-week ceasefire with Iran exists, greeted in Israel with unease rather than relief.
The Iranian regime has hardened, relying on effective state repression tools like arrests, torture, and imprisonment to suppress demonstrations.
The Iranian regime executed 14 people, including 7 linked to January anti-government protests, and imposed a 41-day internet blackout.
Recent protests represent the most serious unrest the Iranian regime has faced, threatening its character.
source: Institute for the Study of War

claim: European powers triggered snapback sanctions against Iran.
